examples.title: Pseudocode: MPI Collective Communication Patterns
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the six main collective communication patterns in MPI: Barrier, Broadcast, Scatter, Gather, Reduce, and Allreduce
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MPI_Collective_Operations
// Demonstrates six fundamental collective communication patterns

// Each function represents a collective where ALL processes must participate

// ============================================
// 1. BARRIER - Synchronization
// ============================================
FUNCTION Barrier(communicator)
 INPUT: communicator (group of processes)
 OUTPUT: None (synchronization point)

 BEGIN
 FOR each process in communicator
 PAUSE execution
 END FOR
 
 // All processes wait until everyone reaches here
 // No data transfer, just synchronization
 RETURN
 END FUNCTION

// ============================================
// 2. BROADCAST - One to All
// ============================================
FUNCTION Broadcast(buffer, count, datatype, root, communicator)
 INPUT: buffer (data), count (number of elements), datatype, root (sender), communicator
 OUTPUT: buffer updated on all processes

 BEGIN
 IF my_rank == root THEN
 // Root process sends data to all
 FOR each process i in communicator WHERE i != root
 SEND buffer to process i
 END FOR
 ELSE
 // Non-root processes receive data
 RECEIVE buffer from root
 END IF
 RETURN
 END FUNCTION

// ============================================
// 3. SCATTER - Distribute Chunks
// ============================================
FUNCTION Scatter(sendbuf, sendcount, sendtype, recvbuf, recvcount, recvtype, root, communicator)
 INPUT: sendbuf (array at root), sendcount (per process), sendtype, recvbuf, recvcount, recvtype, root, communicator
 OUTPUT: Each process receives its chunk

 BEGIN
 FOR each process i in communicator
 chunk_i = portion i of sendbuf
 IF my_rank == root THEN
 SEND chunk_i to process i
 ELSE IF my_rank == i THEN
 RECEIVE chunk_i from root
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN
 END FUNCTION

// ============================================
// 4. GATHER - Collect Data
// ============================================
FUNCTION Gather(sendbuf, sendcount, sendtype, recvbuf, recvcount, recvtype, root, communicator)
 INPUT: sendbuf (local data), sendcount, sendtype, recvbuf (at root), recvcount, recvtype, root, communicator
 OUTPUT: recvbuf contains all data at root

 BEGIN
 IF my_rank != root THEN
 // Non-root processes send their data
 SEND sendbuf to root
 ELSE
 // Root receives from all processes
 FOR each process i in communicator
 RECEIVE data_i from process i
 recvbuf[i] = data_i
 END FOR
 END IF
 RETURN
 END FUNCTION

// ============================================
// 5. REDUCE - Combine with Operation
// ============================================
FUNCTION Reduce(sendbuf, recvbuf, count, datatype, operation, root, communicator)
 INPUT: sendbuf (local values), recvbuf (result), count, datatype, operation (SUM/MAX/MIN), root, communicator
 OUTPUT: Result at root only

 BEGIN
 local_value = sendbuf
 
 // Tree-structured reduction
 temp = local_value
 FOR level = 1 to log2(num_processes)
 partner = my_rank XOR (1 << (level-1))
 IF my_rank has lower bits THEN
 SEND temp to partner
 RECEIVE neighbor_value from partner
 temp = COMBINE(temp, neighbor_value, operation)
 ELSE
 RECEIVE neighbor_value from partner
 SEND temp to partner
 temp = COMBINE(temp, neighbor_value, operation)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF my_rank == root THEN
 recvbuf = temp
 END IF
 RETURN
 END FUNCTION

// ============================================
// 6. ALLREDUCE - Reduce to All
// ============================================
FUNCTION Allreduce(sendbuf, recvbuf, count, datatype, operation, communicator)
 INPUT: sendbuf, recvbuf, count, datatype, operation, communicator
 OUTPUT: All processes receive the reduced result

 BEGIN
 // Same as Reduce but result goes to ALL processes
 temp = sendbuf
 
 // Tree-structured reduction
 FOR level = 1 to log2(num_processes)
 partner = my_rank XOR (1 << (level-1))
 // Exchange and combine values
 SWAP_AND_COMBINE(temp, partner, operation)
 END FOR
 
 recvbuf = temp // All processes have the result
 RETURN
 END FUNCTION

// ============================================
// MAIN PROGRAM DEMONSTRATION
// ============================================
BEGIN Main
 INITIALIZE MPI with argc, argv
 GET my_rank, num_procs
 
 // Example: Calculate global sum of ranks
 local_value = my_rank
 
 // Using Reduce - result only at root (0)
 CALL Reduce(local_value, global_sum, 1, INT, SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD)
 IF my_rank == 0 THEN
 PRINT "Sum of ranks: " + global_sum
 END IF
 
 // Using Allreduce - result at ALL processes
 CALL Allreduce(local_value, global_sum_all, 1, INT, SUM, MPI_COMM_WORLD)
 PRINT "Allreduce result: " + global_sum_all // All processes print
 
 FINALIZE MPI
END Main
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates six fundamental collective communication patterns in MPI. Each operation requires ALL processes in the communicator to call the function with compatible parameters. The Barrier synchronizes processes without data transfer. Broadcast sends data from one root to all. Scatter distributes chunks of an array. Gather collects data to root. Reduce combines values using an operation (like SUM) and stores only at root. Allreduce does the same but distributes result to all processes.

examples.title: Python: MPI Collective Communication with mpi4py
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating all major MPI collective operations using the mpi4py library
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
MPI Collective Communication Operations - Python Implementation
Using mpi4py library

This program demonstrates:
- MPI.Barrier: Synchronization
- MPI.Bcast: Broadcast data from root to all
- MPI.Scatter: Distribute data chunks to all processes
- MPI.Gather: Collect data from all processes to root
- MPI.Reduce: Combine data with operation (result at root)
- MPI.Allreduce: Combine data with operation (result to all)
- MPI.Allgather: Gather data to all processes
"""

from mpi4py import MPI
import numpy as np

def main():
 # Initialize MPI communicator
 comm = MPI.COMM_WORLD
 rank = comm.Get_rank()
 size = comm.Get_size()
 
 print(f"Process {rank}/{size-1} starting...")
 
 # ============================================
 # 1. MPI Barrier - Synchronization
 # ============================================
 # All processes must reach this point before any can proceed
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 1. MPI Barrier (Synchronization) ===")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Each process has a local value
 local_value = rank + 1 # Process 0 has 1, Process 1 has 2, etc.
 print(f"Process {rank}: local_value = {local_value}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 2. MPI Broadcast - One to All
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 2. MPI Broadcast ===")
 print("Root (0) broadcasts data to all processes")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 broadcast_data = None
 if rank == 0:
 broadcast_data = np.array([10, 20, 30, 40])
 
 # Broadcast from root=0 to all processes
 broadcast_data = comm.bcast(broadcast_data, root=0)
 print(f"Process {rank}: received broadcast data = {broadcast_data}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 3. MPI Scatter - Distribute Chunks
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 3. MPI Scatter ===")
 print("Root distributes different chunks to each process")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Create data at root to scatter
 if rank == 0:
 scatter_data = np.arange(size * 2) # [0,1, 2,3, 4,5, 6,7] for 4 procs
 else:
 scatter_data = None
 
 # Each process receives 2 elements
 local_scatter = np.zeros(2, dtype=int)
 comm.Scatter(scatter_data, local_scatter, root=0)
 print(f"Process {rank}: received scatter data = {local_scatter}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 4. MPI Gather - Collect to Root
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 4. MPI Gather ===")
 print("All processes send data to root")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Each process has local data to gather
 gather_send = np.array([rank * 10 + i for i in range(3)]) # [0,1,2], [10,11,12], etc.
 
 # Root receives gathered data
 if rank == 0:
 gather_recv = np.zeros((size, 3), dtype=int)
 else:
 gather_recv = None
 
 comm.Gather(gather_send, gather_recv, root=0)
 
 if rank == 0:
 print(f"Root gathered: {gather_recv}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 5. MPI Reduce - Combine to Root
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 5. MPI Reduce ===")
 print("Sum all local values, result to root only")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Reduce: sum all local_value (1+2+3+4 = 10 for 4 processes)
 total_sum = np.array([0], dtype=int)
 local_val = np.array([local_value], dtype=int)
 comm.Reduce(local_val, total_sum, op=MPI.SUM, root=0)
 
 if rank == 0:
 print(f"Process 0: Global sum = {total_sum[0]}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 6. MPI Allreduce - Combine to All
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 6. MPI Allreduce ===")
 print("Sum all values, result goes to ALL processes")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Allreduce: same as reduce but result goes to everyone
 all_sum = np.array([0], dtype=int)
 comm.Allreduce(local_val, all_sum, op=MPI.SUM)
 print(f"Process {rank}: Allreduce sum = {all_sum[0]}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 7. MPI Allgather - Gather to All
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 7. MPI Allgather ===")
 print("Gather data, then distribute to all")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Each process has one value to contribute
 local_contrib = np.array([rank + 100]) # [100], [101], [102], [103]
 
 # All processes receive all gathered values
 all_gathered = np.zeros(size, dtype=int)
 comm.Allgather(local_contrib, all_gathered)
 print(f"Process {rank}: Allgathered = {all_gathered}")
 
 # ============================================
 # 8. Multiple Reduction Operations
 # ============================================
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== 8. Various Reduction Operations ===")
 comm.Barrier()
 
 test_val = np.array([float(local_value)], dtype=float)
 
 # Test different reduction operations
 results = {}
 for op, name in [(MPI.MAX, "MAX"), (MPI.MIN, "MIN"), 
 (MPI.PROD, "PROD"), (MPI.SUM, "SUM")]:
 result = np.array([0.0], dtype=float)
 comm.Allreduce(test_val, result, op=op)
 results[name] = result[0]
 print(f"Process {rank}: {name} = {results[name]}")
 
 # Final barrier to ensure all complete
 comm.Barrier()
 if rank == 0:
 print("\n=== All operations complete! ===")

if __name__ == "__main__":
 main()
 
# To run this program:
# mpiexec -n 4 python mpi_collective_python.py
# 
# Sample output:
# Process 0/3 starting...
# Process 1/3 starting...
# Process 2/3 starting...
# Process 3/3 starting...
# 
# === 2. MPI Broadcast ===
# Process 0: received broadcast data = [10 20 30 40]
# Process 1: received broadcast data = [10 20 30 40]
# ...
# Process 0: Allreduce sum = 10
# Process 1: Allreduce sum = 10
# Process 2: Allreduce sum = 10
# Process 3: Allreduce sum = 10
examples.explanation: This Python implementation using mpi4py demonstrates all major MPI collective operations. Key points: (1) All processes must call collective functions with compatible parameters, (2) Broadcast sends the same data to all, (3) Scatter sends different chunks to each process, (4) Gather collects to root while Allgather distributes to all, (5) Reduce combines with an operation and stores at root, (6) Allreduce does the same but all processes receive the result. The program uses numpy arrays for efficient data handling and shows various reduction operations (SUM, MAX, MIN, PROD).
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) for tree-based collectives, O(n) for linear implementations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for gathered results, O(1) per process for simple operations

examples.title: C: MPI Collective Communication Operations
examples.description: C implementation demonstrating all major MPI collective operations with practical examples
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides comprehensive coverage of MPI collective operations. The code demonstrates: (1) MPI_Bcast for broadcasting data from root to all, (2) MPI_Scatter for distributing different chunks to each process, (3) MPI_Gather for collecting data to root, (4) MPI_Reduce for combining values with an operation and storing at root, (5) MPI_Allreduce for distributing the reduced result to all processes, (6) MPI_Allgather for gathering and distributing to all. It also includes practical examples showing parallel sum reduction, global maximum finding, and matrix-vector multiplication using collective operations. The program uses MPI_SUM, MPI_MAX and other reduction operators to show different aggregation possibilities.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) for tree-based implementations, O(n) for linear gather/scatter
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for gather/Allgather buffers, O(1) for simple reductions
